text.title: Claude Best Practices Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns most often observed when productionising Claude pipelines. Each entry has a symptom (`description`), root cause (`reason`), and corrective fix (`note`).

antipattern.description: Cache hit ratio sits below 50% in production despite a "stable" system prompt.
antipattern.reason: Dynamic injection (timestamps, request ids, JSON with shifting key order) lives before the cached block.
antipattern.note: Fix: move all dynamic content into the user turn or strictly after the cached block; lock JSON key order; assert 70%+ on a rolling window.

antipattern.description: RateLimitError waits look erratic; cluster of failures clustered immediately after the backoff window.
antipattern.reason: Caller is guessing the backoff interval instead of parsing the `retry-after` header; multiple workers backoff in lockstep.
antipattern.note: Fix: read `retry-after` from the exception headers and sleep that value exactly; use a shared token bucket across workers.

antipattern.description: Quality regression after a quiet outage; cause unknown.
antipattern.reason: Fallback path silently switched from Sonnet to Haiku without logging the new model.
antipattern.note: Fix: log `response.model` on every call; alert on tier drift; fix the rate-limit source rather than masking it.

antipattern.description: Structured-output calls return malformed JSON intermittently.
antipattern.reason: `max_tokens` too low; `stop_reason==max_tokens` is treated as a parsing failure instead of a truncation signal.
antipattern.note: Fix: branch on `stop_reason==max_tokens` BEFORE attempting `json.loads`; retry the call with a higher budget.

antipattern.reason: Cost tracker counts each retry attempt as a billable call.
antipattern.note: Fix: bind cost tracking to the OUTER decorated function exit, not each attempt; track only successful + final-failed calls.
